name: cc-skill-continuous-learning
qualityReason: Highly relevant and specific frontend guidance
capabilities: Monitors Claude Code sessions via the Stop hook for efficient execution
Detects patterns including error resolution, debugging techniques, and workarounds
Filters out noise like simple typos and one-time fixes
Extracts reusable knowledge from sessions exceeding minimum message threshold
Saves learned skills to the local ~/.claude/skills/learned directory
Configurable extraction threshold and pattern detection rules
author: affaan-m
securitySummary: All 19 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be FALSE POSITIVES. Hidden file paths reference the standard ~/.claude/skills directory for skill storage. Shell command substitutions use hardcoded arguments with no user input injection vectors. The weak cryptographic algorithm finding on SKILL.md lines 3 and 15 is incorrect as those lines contain only descriptive text. The skill performs legitimate session evaluation and local file operations within the Claude Code ecosystem.
valueStatement: Stop repeating the same solutions. This skill captures your debugging techniques and workflow improvements automatically, turning each Claude Code session into lasting knowledge for future projects.
userTitle: Extract reusable patterns from Claude Code sessions
limitations: Requires sessions with at least 10 messages to trigger evaluation
Only processes sessions when Claude Code exits cleanly via the Stop hook
Does not capture patterns from external API issues or third-party service problems
Extracted skills are stored locally and do not sync across machines automatically

useCases.targetUser: Solo developers working on multiple projects
useCases.title: Individual developer building personal knowledge base
useCases.description: Automatically capture debugging workflows and project-specific solutions that recur across development sessions

useCases.title: Team standardizing best practices
useCases.description: Extract consistent patterns from team members sessions to build shared skill libraries for onboarding

useCases.title: Complex project documentation
useCases.description: Document project-specific troubleshooting steps and architectural decisions through captured patterns

promptTemplates.title: Review my recent debugging session
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the last Claude Code session and identify any reusable debugging patterns that should be saved as a skill. Focus on error resolution techniques that could apply to future sessions.
promptTemplates.scenario: After completing a complex debugging task

promptTemplates.title: Extract workflow improvements
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this session for any workflow improvements or workarounds discovered. Save patterns that would be valuable to remember for similar projects in the future.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have found efficient solutions to recurring problems

promptTemplates.title: Capture project-specific knowledge
promptTemplates.prompt: This session contained project-specific knowledge about [project name]. Extract any patterns related to our codebase structure, common issues, or architectural decisions that should be preserved.
promptTemplates.scenario: After deep work on a specific codebase

promptTemplates.title: Build a reusable skill from multiple sessions
promptTemplates.prompt: I have completed several sessions working on [topic]. Review the patterns across these sessions and create a comprehensive skill that captures the key learnings and techniques discovered.
promptTemplates.scenario: After completing a multi-session learning or development effort

bestPractices: Set an appropriate minimum session length in config.json to avoid capturing trivial interactions
Review extracted skills periodically to ensure quality and relevance before relying on them
Use specific pattern names in your sessions to help the skill categorize learnings accurately
antiPatterns: Do not set extraction threshold too low or you will accumulate low-value skills
Avoid using this skill for one-off problems that will never recur in your work
Do not rely solely on automatic extraction for critical knowledge documentation

faq.question: How does the skill know when to extract patterns?
faq.answer: The skill runs automatically when Claude Code exits via the Stop hook. It checks if your session has enough messages and signals for evaluation if patterns are detected.

faq.question: Where are the extracted skills stored?
faq.answer: Skills are saved to ~/.claude/skills/learned/ by default. You can customize this path in the config.json file.

faq.question: Can I configure what patterns get detected?
faq.answer: Yes, edit the patterns_to_detect and ignore_patterns arrays in config.json to customize what the skill looks for.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all Claude Code versions?
faq.answer: This skill requires Claude Code with hook support. Check your Claude Code version and settings.json for hook configuration options.

faq.question: What happens if a session is too short?
faq.answer: Sessions with fewer messages than min_session_length in config.json are skipped to avoid capturing trivial or incomplete interactions.

faq.question: Can I share extracted skills with my team?
faq.answer: The skills are stored locally. You can manually copy them from ~/.claude/skills/learned/ to share with teammates or set up a shared directory.
